No known drug allergies 1. Preoperative Assessment Medications Reviewed and no changes noted HPI: This is a 78 year old male who: February 16, 2026. 1. is being seen for a preoperative evaluation. He is scheduled for a right mako assisted anterior total hip allopurinol 300 mg Oral - tablet atorvastatin 10 mg Oral - tablet arthroplasty on 02/26/2026, at Ash. Eliquis 2.5 mg Oral - tablet Frequency: BID gabapentin 300 mg Oral - capsule Patient is coming in for his preop exam. He is scheduled proceed with a right anterior total hip arthroplasty. Patient lisinopril 10 mg Oral tablet notes that he continues to have significant right hip pain that is affecting his activities of daily living. He has previously metoprolol tartrate 25 mg Oral - tablet tried and failed conservative treatment and wishes to proceed with surgery as scheduled. Of note patient he is on omeprazole 20 mg Oral - Eliquis and is to stop it 48 hours prior to surgery per his primary care. capsule,delayed release (e.c.) Wegovy 2.4 mg/0.75 mL Subcutaneous Pen Injector Exam: Medical History Reviewed and no changes noted February 16, 2026. Blood clot: In lung Exam Chronic pain: Back Diabetes mellitus Alert and oriented to person, place and time. In no acute distress. Vital signs are stable. Ambulates. No obvious Elevated blood pressure Hypercholesterolemia lymphedema is present. Musculoskeletal History No changes from previous exam. Reviewed and no changes noted February 16, 2026. Left handed: operation HEENT: Normal Heart: Normal Musculoskeletal Surgery Repair of meniscus: Rt & Lt knee Lungs: Normal Other: Rt & Lt shoulder rotary cup operation Skin: normal Neurological: Sensation grossly intact/ Normal. Surgical History Reviewed and no changes noted February 16, 2026. Cataract surgery: Both eyes Repair of multiple tears of rotator cuff Impression/Plan: of left shoulder 1. Hip Osteoarthritis, Right (Patient Management Risk Level: Low) Repair of multiple tears of rotator cuff / feel the patient's right hip symptoms and previous imaging are severe enough to proceed with a right total hip of right shoulder Other: Left & Right Meniscus Repair arthroplasty as scheduled. Risks and benefits of the proposed procedure were discussed in detail with the patient. L3-L5 Laminectomy Complications can include infection, blood clots, nerve artery vein tendon or bone injury, continued pain, the need Spacer in L3 & L4 for further surgery amongst other things. The patient did express understanding, all questions were answered. We Right Carpal Tunnel Release discussed what type of implants will be used. Patient has increased VTE risk and will be placed back on his Eliquis following surgery for DVT and prior heart Social History Reviewed and no changes noted issues. February 16, 2026. Single Question Alcohol Screening: 1 Clearance has been received and reviewed today on patient. He is able to proceed with surgery as scheduled. days Unilateral primary osteoarthritis, right hip (M16.11) Exercise: Unspecified Status: Worsening Occupation: Retired EtOH less than 1 drink per day: Occasionally Plan: Counseling - Preoperative Instructions. textSpans.text: Patient is coming in for his preop exam.
/ feel the patient's right hip symptoms and previous imaging are severe enough to proceed with a right total hip
Risks and benefits of the proposed procedure were discussed in detail with the patient.
The patient did express understanding, all questions were answered.
Clearance has been received and reviewed today on patient.
Instructions: evaluate and treat per diagnosis/objective exam, post-operative protocol, and home health
Complications can include infection, blood clots, nerve artery vein tendon or bone injury, continued pain, the need
Alert and oriented to person, place and time.
Hip Osteoarthritis, Right (Patient Management Risk Level: Low)
Patient has increased VTE risk and will be placed back on his Eliquis following surgery for DVT and prior heart

textSpans.text: Indication: Hip Osteoarthritis, Right - right hip joint - M.16.11
Recommend frequency of 2-3 times per week for 6-8;weeks.
- Therapeutic Exercises: All exercises prn per therapist.
- Manual Therapy: All manual therapy prn per therapist.
- Modalities: All modalities prn per therapist.
HPI: This is a 78 year old male who:
1. is being seen for a preoperative evaluation.
He is scheduled for a right mako assisted anterior total hip
He is scheduled proceed with a right anterior total hip arthroplasty.
notes that he continues to have significant right hip pain that is affecting his activities of daily living.
tried and failed conservative treatment and wishes to proceed with surgery as scheduled.
Of note patient he is on
Eliquis and is to stop it 48 hours prior to surgery per his primary care.
Repair of meniscus: Rt & Lt knee
Other: Rt & Lt shoulder rotary cup
Repair of multiple tears of rotator cuff
for further surgery amongst other things.
discussed what type of implants will be used.
He is able to proceed with surgery as scheduled.
EtOH less than 1 drink per day:
Please refer to the education handout for detailed counseling.
Drives in the Daytime: Grocery doctor
Electronically Signed By: Karlene Gonzales, PA-C, 02/27/2026 12:10 PM MST
Electronically Signed By: Karlene Gonzales, PA-C, 02/16/2026 02:41 PM MST

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: Ralph E. Pierce, a 78-year-old male, is being admitted to home health care following a recent right total hip arthroplasty performed on February 26, 2026. This surgical intervention was necessitated by significant pain that impaired his activities of daily living, despite previous conservative treatments. Post-operatively, Mr. Pierce demonstrates functional limitations, including decreased mobility and difficulty ambulating without assistance, necessitating a structured rehabilitation program.
